risk: Increased bleeding risk due to additive anticoagulant effects
mechanism: Ginkgo inhibits platelet-activating factor (PAF) and may potentiate warfarin's anticoagulant activity
recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. If combined, monitor INR closely and watch for signs of bleeding.

risk: Increased bleeding risk including spontaneous hemorrhage
mechanism: Both agents inhibit platelet aggregation through different mechanisms, creating additive effect
recommendation: Avoid combination. Case reports of spontaneous hyphema and subdural hematoma.

mechanism: St. John's Wort inhibits serotonin reuptake, causing additive serotonergic effects with SSRIs
recommendation: Contraindicated. Do not combine with any SSRI. Taper one before starting the other.

mechanism: Additive serotonergic activity from dual reuptake inhibition
recommendation: Contraindicated. Choose one treatment modality.

risk: Reduced cyclosporine levels leading to transplant rejection
mechanism: St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, accelerating cyclosporine metabolism
recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated in transplant patients. Multiple cases of acute rejection reported.

risk: Breakthrough bleeding and reduced contraceptive efficacy
mechanism: CYP3A4 induction increases metabolism of ethinyl estradiol and progestins
recommendation: Use additional barrier contraception or choose alternative antidepressant.

risk: Enhanced anticoagulant effect and increased bleeding risk
mechanism: Curcumin inhibits platelet aggregation and may inhibit thromboxane synthase
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. Avoid high-dose curcumin supplements while on warfarin.

risk: Additive blood glucose lowering — risk of hypoglycemia
mechanism: Curcumin may enhance insulin sensitivity and reduce hepatic glucose production
recommendation: Monitor blood glucose more frequently. Adjust metformin dose if needed.

risk: May increase thyroid hormone levels excessively
mechanism: Ashwagandha stimulates thyroid activity and may increase T3/T4 production
recommendation: Monitor thyroid function tests. May need dose adjustment of levothyroxine.

mechanism: Ashwagandha has GABAergic activity that may potentiate benzodiazepine effects
recommendation: Use with caution. Reduce sedative dose if combining. Avoid alcohol.

mechanism: Ashwagandha has immunostimulatory properties that may oppose immunosuppression
recommendation: Avoid in transplant patients or autoimmune conditions requiring immunosuppression.

mechanism: Ginsenosides enhance insulin secretion and improve insulin sensitivity
recommendation: Monitor blood glucose closely. May need to reduce insulin dose.

mechanism: Ginseng may have antiplatelet properties that paradoxically reduce warfarin's INR effect
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. Avoid concurrent use if possible.

mechanism: Valerian acts on GABA-A receptors, potentiating benzodiazepine sedation
recommendation: Avoid combination or use reduced doses. Do not drive or operate machinery.

mechanism: Both act on GABA receptors, creating additive CNS depression
recommendation: Do not combine. Choose one sleep aid.

risk: Hypokalemia increasing risk of digoxin toxicity and fatal arrhythmia
mechanism: Glycyrrhizin causes pseudoaldosteronism with potassium depletion, sensitizing heart to digoxin
recommendation: Contraindicated. Avoid licorice root (not deglycyrrhizinated form) with digoxin.

mechanism: Both licorice and thiazides deplete potassium through different mechanisms
recommendation: Monitor potassium levels closely. Supplement potassium if combining.

mechanism: Garlic inhibits platelet aggregation via ajoene and allicin, adding to warfarin's effect
recommendation: Monitor INR. Avoid high-dose garlic supplements while on anticoagulants.

mechanism: Garlic induces CYP3A4 and intestinal P-glycoprotein, reducing drug absorption
recommendation: Avoid garlic supplements with protease inhibitors. Risk of treatment failure.

risk: Increased phenytoin blood levels and toxicity risk
mechanism: Piperine inhibits CYP3A4 and CYP2C9, slowing phenytoin metabolism
recommendation: Monitor phenytoin levels. Avoid high-dose piperine/bioperine supplements.

mechanism: Ginger may inhibit thromboxane synthase, affecting platelet aggregation
recommendation: Culinary amounts are likely safe. Avoid concentrated ginger supplements with anticoagulants.

mechanism: Cinnamon may improve insulin sensitivity and reduce fasting glucose
recommendation: Monitor blood glucose. Beneficial in some cases but adjust medications accordingly.

mechanism: Neem leaf extract may reduce blood glucose through multiple mechanisms
recommendation: Monitor blood glucose closely when using neem supplements with antidiabetics.

mechanism: Giloy is a potent immunomodulator that activates macrophages and lymphocytes
recommendation: Avoid in transplant patients or those on immunosuppressive therapy.

mechanism: Eugenol in tulsi has mild antiplatelet activity
recommendation: Monitor INR. Culinary use is likely safe; avoid concentrated extracts.
